things.name: Newgrange & Brú na Bóinne (UNESCO)
things.description: 5,200-year-old passage tomb older than Stonehenge and the Great Pyramid — a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Ireland's most important prehistoric site

things.name: Millmount Museum & Tower
things.description: Drogheda's Martello tower museum on a prehistoric mound — local history collections and superb views over the town and Boyne valley

things.name: St Peter's Church (Oliver Plunkett Shrine)
things.description: The preserved head of St Oliver Plunkett, martyred Archbishop of Armagh — one of Ireland's most visited pilgrimage sites

things.name: Monasterboice Round Tower & High Crosses
things.description: 5th-century monastic site with one of Ireland's finest round towers and the intricately carved Muiredach's High Cross — a masterpiece of Celtic art

things.name: Battle of the Boyne Site (Oldbridge Estate)
things.description: Site of the 1690 battle where William of Orange defeated James II — a beautifully restored estate with an excellent visitor centre

things.name: Drogheda Town Walls (Laurence Gate)
things.description: Drogheda's impressive medieval walls — Laurence Gate is one of Ireland's finest surviving medieval town gates, built in the 13th century

things.name: Knowth & Dowth (Brú na Bóinne)
things.description: Sister passage tombs to Newgrange in the Boyne Valley — Knowth has more decorated megalithic art than any site in Western Europe

things.name: Slane Castle
things.description: Gothic Revival castle of the Marquess of Conyngham in the beautiful Boyne valley — famous for hosting major rock concerts

things.name: Mellifont Abbey (Day Trip)
things.description: Ireland's first Cistercian abbey founded in 1142 — the elegant Romanesque Lavabo is one of Ireland's finest medieval architectural survivors

things.name: Termonfeckin Heritage Village
things.description: Charming coastal village with a 15th-century tower house, thatched pub and a Blue Flag beach on the Irish Sea
